top_red_flags: The NLU failure on 'Qualquer hora' (Anytime) broke the illusion of speaking to a human and forced the customer to adapt to the machine's rigid constraints.
Sluggish turn-taking latency (p50 of 1.44s) made the conversation feel laggy.
Ignoring the customer's clear constraint ('Só recebo dia 30') and immediately asking 'what time would be good to pay' makes the bot sound completely deaf to user input.
Severe turn-taking delays exceeding 3 seconds create dead-air moments that ruin the conversational illusion.
The 15-second loop of false-interruption prompts ('Pode falar', 'Estou ouvindo') at 4:05 completely breaks the human illusion.
A 10.9-second silent delay during the identity verification phase.
A massive 4.9-second silence before repeating a question the customer had already answered.
Gender mismatch where female agent 'Laura' says 'Fico preocupado' (masculine form).
The 36-second continuous monologue at the end of the call completely destroys the illusion of a human conversation.
The agent repeated the exact same 50% discount offer twice in a row, ignoring the customer's clear statement of financial difficulty.
A double-start audio overlap occurred at 0:44, exposing the synthetic nature of the voice platform.
The bot repeated the customer's own words ('Iya bener ini saya suaminya') back to him at 0:13.
Addressing a male speaker as 'Ibu' (ma'am) multiple times throughout the call.
Severe double-talk/overlap lasting nearly 95 seconds.
A 9.2-second dead-air gap that completely breaks the conversational flow and forces the customer to ask if anyone is still there.
Verbatim repetition of the question 'pembayarannya akan dibayarkan jam berapa ya?' which immediately exposes the agent as a bot.
Ignoring the customer's 'secepatnya' (as soon as possible) response and repeating the exact same 'pay today' demand, exposing the bot's lack of true comprehension.
The 4.4-second processing delay at 1:13 creates an awkward silence that breaks conversational flow.
Ignoring the customer's 'no me pagaron' (I didn't get paid) explanation to push an immediate payment is a major customer experience and anthropomorphic failure.
Empathy hallucination: Agent sympathized with a 'health issue' at 0:38 before the customer actually revealed his wife was in the hospital at 0:48.
Name mispronunciation: Repeatedly calling the customer 'Jugo' instead of 'Hugo'.
Double-triggering TTS glitch at 1:28, causing overlapping agent voices.
Extremely long and tedious IVR menu at the start of the call, which ruins the conversational flow.
Agent answers its own identity verification prompt by saying 'Satu, dua, tiga, empat' (1, 2, 3, 4).
Agent answers its own closing pitch by saying 'Berminat' (Interested) to force-complete the call flow.
Severe repetition loop where the agent repeats the entire opening pitch back-to-back from 0:25 to 0:48.
A massive 7.6-second delay before the agent's turn at 1:10.
The agent repeated its entire introduction script at 0:31, completely ignoring the previous context.
A 2.19-second delay before the opening greeting immediately breaks the illusion of a human agent.
16 overlap events totaling 27.3 seconds of simultaneous speech/channel bleeding, indicating a major VAD or echo-cancellation failure.
2.25-second initial delay before the first word.
25.68 seconds of overlapping speech due to broken barge-in detection.
Incoherent, garbled script in the final agent turn ('since sampai hal of yatim...').
A 10-second dead-air silence at 3:04 before asking 'Are you still there?'.
Extremely long, uninterrupted 39-second sales pitch monologue at the start of the call.
Excessive turn-taking latency (exceeding 2 seconds) which breaks the conversational flow and clearly exposes the agent as an AI.
A complete lack of verbal fillers or backchannels during longer customer explanations.
rows.type: Collection 3.0 双语 英文+中文

top_red_flags: Extremely high response latency (p50 1830 ms) which immediately breaks the illusion of talking to a human.
Severe double-talk and overlap issues (10 events, 5.5 seconds total) due to poor barge-in handling.
rows.type: Collection 3.0 三语 英文+中文+马来语

top_red_flags: Severe channel overlap (64.7 seconds of double-talk) showing a lack of proper barge-in control.
Highly repetitive 'Thank you, [Name]. Could I also have your [Entity]?' loop during data collection.
Severe repetition loop at 0:41-0:51 ('Pay Maya Paymaya or... Confirmation information Thank you...').
An 8.8-second dead-air gap between 'Actually' and 'Thank you for the confirmation'.
Extremely fragmented sentence structure throughout the call.
13.8-second dead-air delay before the agent's final turn.
19 overlap events causing severe double-talk and confusion.
Extremely fragmented, single-word utterances ('Sorry', 'Okay', 'Payment') that fail to form coherent sentences.
Introducing the virtual assistant 43 seconds into the call.
Reading raw database string formatting ('Firstcharacteremailletterl...') out loud.
Over 20 seconds of cumulative dead air during a 93-second call.
The bot answers its own question ('สะดวกรับวันนี้ไหมค่ะ อ่า สะดวกค่ะ') within a single continuous turn.
12 seconds of total overlapping speech where the bot completely ignored the customer's voice.
A massive 2.7-second silent gap in the middle of a sentence while retrieving the COD amount.
The bot hallucinated the year as 2526 BE (1983 AD), which is a major compliance and conversational red flag.
42 overlap events totaling 36 seconds of double-talk, making the conversation sound chaotic and highly robotic.
27-second initial delay before the first word is spoken.
Severe language contamination, outputting Japanese characters ('僕、門限') in a Vietnamese market call.
Extremely high turn-taking latency exceeding 10 seconds near the end of the call.
The agent repeated the identity confirmation prompt 3 times despite the customer saying 'Yes, sir' on the first prompt.
Over 37 seconds of total dead air in an 88-second call.
A massive 86-second dead-air gap between 0:46 and 2:12.
A clear LLM prompt leak/hallucination at 2:12 where the bot speaks its own instructions ('ช่วยแนะนำบริการของบริษัทให้หน่อยนะคะ').
An incredibly rude and robotic hard-timeout hangup at 2:51 citing that the customer exceeded the allowed call duration.
Addressing a male customer ('Nguyễn Hữu Tài') as 'chị' (ma'am).
Plowing through a severe overlap at 0:44 without stopping or acknowledging the interruption.
Ending the call abruptly with a goodbye script without waiting for the customer's final response.
A 4.7-second mid-sentence dead-air gap at 0:27.
Hallucinating an absurdly high shipping fee of 20 million VND for local delivery to Hoc Mon.
Messy overlap at 0:44 where the bot plows through the customer's speech.
A 12-second continuous legal disclaimer at the very beginning of the call.
25.4 seconds of total overlap/barge-in issues in a 36-second call.
A 2.7-second dead-air gap before the agent responds to the customer's first inquiry.
A 15-second legal disclaimer dumped immediately at the start of the call.
A 7.7-second dead-air gap before the agent confirmed the customer's details.
Severe diarization and overlap issues where both speakers spoke over each other.

notes: Canonical 32-file index used; raw folder scan intentionally avoided.
Deepgram STT plus Zenlayer gemini-3.5-flash text scoring used for this round.
